ipo.headline: De-dollarisation, war, and debt: Why gold is regaining monetary relevance
ipo.summary: Gold is regaining prominence as global markets face geopolitical tensions, rising debt and de-dollarisation trends. Central bank buying, structural shifts in the financial system and weakening confidence in fiat currencies are driving demand. Despite short-term volatility, gold’s long-term outlook remains strong amid a transition towards a multipolar global economic order.

ipo.headline: Elon Musk’s SpaceX eyes over $2 trillion valuation in upcoming IPO
ipo.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX is reportedly targeting an IPO valuation exceeding $2 trillion, potentially making it the largest stock market listing ever. The company is in discussions with prospective investors, aiming to raise up to $75 billion. This ambitious valuation follows a recent merger with Musk's AI startup, xAI.

ipo.headline: EPC player Sadbhav Futuretech files draft papers with Sebi for IPO
ipo.summary: EPC solar player Sadbhav Futuretech has filed its draft papers with Sebi for an IPO comprising a fresh issue and an OFS. The company plans to use the proceeds for working capital, while financials show strong revenue and profit growth ahead of the proposed listing by Beeline Capital Advisors.

ipo.headline: Clock ticks for a dozen Indian IPOs as approvals near expiry
ipo.summary: India's IPO market faces potential disruptions as several companies risk missing their listing deadlines due to a market downturn. Firms like Credila Financial Services and Dorf-Ketal Chemicals are among those with approvals nearing expiry. Valuation concerns and weak secondary market performance are prompting companies to delay or refile, impacting the IPO pipeline after two strong years.

ipo.headline: Core4 Engineers files draft papers with BSE's SME platform; to raise funds via IPO
ipo.summary: Core4 Engineers is preparing for its Initial Public Offering on the BSE SME platform. The company plans to raise funds through a fresh issue of equity shares. These funds will be used for acquiring property to establish a new manufacturing unit and corporate office. Additionally, the capital will support incremental working capital needs and general corporate purposes.

nifty.headline: Eternal, SBI, Bharti Airtel, 6 other stocks can rally up to 57%. How many do you have?
nifty.summary: Axis Direct has identified nine large-cap stocks, including SBI, Bharti Airtel and Bajaj Finance, with upside potential of up to 57%, while pegging the Nifty target at 29,480 by December 2026. The picks are backed by strong fundamentals, growth visibility and sectoral tailwinds despite recent market underperformance.

nifty.headline: Q4 impact: Bank stocks slump up to 32% in 3 months, but brokerages bet on SBI, HDFC Bank, 6 more stocks. Check why
nifty.summary: Banking stocks have slumped up to 32% over three months, underperforming the Nifty amid FII outflows, rising oil prices and rate concerns. Brokerages remain selective, backing SBI, ICICI Bank and others despite margin pressure. Q4 results and FY27 outlook will be key triggers for the sector’s recovery.

nifty.headline: Axis picks 5 smallcap stocks that can rally up to 60%. How many are in your portfolio?
nifty.summary: Axis Direct has set a Nifty target of 29,480 for December 2026, indicating 30% upside, and identified five smallcap stocks with potential gains of up to 60%. Picks include Ujjivan Small Finance Bank, Chalet Hotels and Minda Corporation, backed by improving fundamentals, strong order books and favourable sector outlook.

nifty.headline: Raamdeo Agrawal's portfolio is down 17–18% — and he says this is not the time to panic
nifty.summary: MOFSL Chairman Raamdeo Agrawal advises investors to prioritize survival during the current West Asia conflict, which he finds harder to predict than Covid. He notes that while India's macro resilience has improved significantly, valuations are becoming attractive with the Nifty below 20 PE, suggesting the market is pricing in fear rather than an earnings collapse.

nifty.headline: Indices plunge 2%, then ride rupee rebound to log gains
nifty.summary: Indian benchmark indices rebounded on Thursday, closing higher after a rupee recovery spurred short covering ahead of an extended weekend. Despite early losses, Sensex and Nifty ended in positive territory, contrasting with global market weakness. Volatility remains a concern, though technical indicators suggest a potential near-term pullback.

nifty.headline: Sensex rises 185 points, Nifty reclaims 22,700 on value buying, rupee rebound
nifty.summary: A sharp rebound in the rupee after the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) stepped in with a slew of measures to restrict banks from onshore forward markets also helped improve investor sentiment

nifty.headline: Stock Market Crash Today Highlights: Nifty, Sensex Log Sixth Consecutive Weekly Losses Despite Thursday's Gain; Brent Tops $108/Barrel
nifty.summary: Fourteen out of fifteen sectoral gauges compiled by the NSE trade lower, led by the NSE Nifty PSU Bank Index's 2.4% losses. On the flipside, the NSE Nifty IT Index was the only sectoral gainer, up 1.5%

nifty.headline: Sensex rebounds 1,800 pts from day’s low, settles in green; Nifty above 22,700 as rupee strengthens
nifty.summary: Indian stock markets experienced significant volatility on Thursday, with a sharp crash triggered by US President Trump's comments on Iran. Despite an initial plunge of over 1,600 points for Sensex and 500 points for Nifty 50, both indices ultimately closed higher for the second consecutive session, marking marginal gains.

nifty.headline: Nifty’s December bull case target at 29,480, says Axis Direct; names 15 stocks to ride Iran war-driven volatility
nifty.summary: Indian equities underperformed in 2025–26 due to global headwinds, FII outflows, and rising oil prices, despite improving domestic earnings and policy support. March 2026 saw a sharp correction amid geopolitical tensions. Axis Direct recommends high-quality stocks, advises maintaining 10-15% liquidity, and expects FY27 to offer buying opportunities, supported by a favorable base, structural reforms, and long-term corporate earnings growth.

nifty.headline: “Focus on quality, buy in stages”: Dharmesh Kant’s strategy for uncertain market
nifty.summary: Investors face a challenging market driven by geopolitical headlines, making decisive calls difficult. Dharmesh Kant suggests a scenario-based approach for the Nifty, with valuations reflecting realism. He advises focusing on quality stocks and staggered buying, highlighting defence as a strong sector.

iran.headline: US-Israel-Iran War Live News Updates: Indian Family Seeks Return of Sailor's Remains; Kuwait's Mina al-Ahmadi Oil Refinery Hit
iran.summary: Israel faces incoming fire from Iran amid ongoing Middle East conflict, with Kuwait and Bahrain also reporting attacks, highlighting regional tensions.

iran.headline: Middle-East Crisis: Government Plans Rs 2 Lakh Crore Credit Scheme To Support Sectors Hit By US-Iran Conflict
iran.summary: The government is preparing a large credit support plan to cushion sectors facing emerging stress, signalling a pre-emptive move to manage potential economic impact due to Iran war.

iran.headline: US Stocks | US stocks end mixed as Iran war worries linger before Holiday break
iran.summary: U.S. stocks closed mixed on Thursday, paring earlier losses as diplomatic efforts in the Middle East eased concerns over oil supply disruptions. Investor sentiment steadied after Iran and Britain signaled de-escalation, despite earlier jitters from President Trump's threats. Utilities rose, while consumer discretionary stocks slid.

iran.headline: US-Israel-Iran War News Updates: : Hormuz Strait Disruptions Caused Energy Instability, Severe Economic Impact In Arabian Sea Region
iran.summary: Trump's speech will offer a wide audience to articulate clear objectives for the war that could attempt to reconcile weeks of changing goals and often contradictory messages about whether he's winding down or ready to escalate military operations

iran.headline: 'Time For Iran To Make Deal Before It's Too Late': Trump's Fresh Warning As Key Bridge In Tehran Hit
iran.summary: Over the past few days, Trump has repeatedly shifted his stance on the Iran war, at times hinting at an early end to the conflict and at others talking about hitting Iran hard.

iran.headline: Iran Plans To Team Up With Oman To Monitor Traffic In Hormuz As Trump Comes Under Global Pressure
iran.summary: The passage is officially international waters and any attempt by Iran to assert control over traffic would be opposed by Western powers and Gulf Arab states.

iran.headline: Hormuz Disruptions Impact Economy And Energy Supply: Navy Chief
iran.summary: The crisis in West Asia began following a joint attack by the United States and Israel on Iran on February 28.

iran.headline: Iran's Bridge Connecting Tehran to Karaj Struck After Trump Vows Extremely Hard' Action
iran.summary: Several people were injured in the strike, which was carried out by the United States and Israel, Iranian state media reported.

iran.headline: Hormuz Cheque: Iran Deputy FM Confirms Plan To 'Set Tolls' For Ships
iran.summary: The move comes against a backdrop of sharply heightened regional tensions, with shipping via the Strait of Hormuz already disrupted.

iran.headline: Food Bills Set To Soar? Restaurants May Raise Menu Prices As Iran War Hits Kitchen Costs
iran.summary: According to HRAWI spokesperson Pradeep Shetty, the sector has been under sustained pressure since the conflict began in late February.

iran.headline: Gold futures drops nearly 4% to ₹1.47 lakh/10g as Trump's Iran warning lifts dollar
iran.summary: Analysts said precious metal prices declined as the U.S. dollar rebounded following remarks by President Donald Trump indicating a potential escalation in the ongoing conflict with Iran

iran.headline: US Stock Market Today: Nasdaq Plummets 2%, Dow Down 600 Points As Trump's Iran Speech Rattles Wall Street
iran.summary: The wider index, S&P 500, slipped 1.4% to hit a low of 6,495 minutes after the opening bell.

iran.headline: What Is 'Greater Israel'? Origins, History And Why The Debate Has Resurfaced Amid Iran-US Conflict
iran.summary: The debate has resurfaced following Israel’s recent ground offensive into southern Lebanon, aimed at pushing back Hezbollah forces and creating a buffer zone along its northern border.

iran.headline: Bring It On! Iran's Warning After Trump's Speech: 'Iranians Don't Talk Of Defending Country, They Bleed'
iran.summary: In a strongly worded statement posted on X, Iran's Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf invoked personal loss and national pride to send a message of resistance.

iran.headline: Iran's 'Oldest Research Centre' Turned Into Rubble: Tehran Hits Out At US-Israel For 'Barbaric Assault'
iran.summary: "The American-Israeli aggressors have attacked the Pasteur Institute of Iran — the oldest and most prestigious research and public health center in Iran," Iran's foreign ministry said.

iran.headline: US Stock Market Today | Dow Jones | Nasdaq Live: US stocks futures slide as Trump threatens more strikes on Iran
Iran War Hits Pakistan Economy, Inflation At 17-Month High; Fuel Prices Up By Nearly A Fourth
iran.summary: Rising oil shock from the Iran war squeezes Pakistan, as IMF curbs relief and households bear the brunt of soaring fuel costs.

iran.headline: Gold slides 3% as oil surges on dimming hopes of end to Iran war
iran.summary: Gold prices dropped sharply on Thursday, pulling back from ​two-week highs, as oil prices ​surged after U.S. President Donald Trump vowed continued attacks on ​Iran, fuelling inflation concerns and reducing U.S. rate-cut bets.

iran.headline: Wall Street Set For Mayhem? S&P 500, Nasdaq Futures Plunge As Trump Dashes Iran Ceasefire Hopes
iran.summary: Tech-heavy Nasdaq futures drop nearly 2% as geopolitical tensions flare, signalling a gap-down start for the US stock market.

iran.headline: Beyond Iran war, what drove sharp equity selloff in March? Aswath Damodaran explains
iran.summary: Global markets saw sharp volatility in March as the Iran war and a surge in crude oil prices reshaped investor expectations. Aswath Damodaran said the selloff reflected recalibration, not panic. Rising inflation expectations, higher bond yields and modest risk repricing drove equities lower amid continued uncertainty over the conflict’s trajectory.

iran.headline: Iran War Impact: HSBC's Pranjul Bhandari More Worried About India's Growth Than Inflation
iran.summary: HSBC Chief India Economist Pranjul Bhandari warns that natural gas and LPG shortages could severely drag down India's economic growth over the coming year.

iran.headline: Middle-East Crisis: Gas Supply Loss May Persist Even If US-Iran War Ends, Oil Markets Stay Tight: FGE's Nasseri
iran.summary: Oil markets remain under pressure due to reduced flows from the Gulf, particularly through the Strait of Hormuz, a key transit route for global energy supplies.

iran.headline: Oil could hit $140 if Iran tensions escalate, warns Peter McGuire; here's what happens to gold next
iran.summary: Peter McGuire of Australia-Trading.com highlights how Trump's Iran ultimatum has created market volatility, particularly in oil, gold, and silver. He outlines two oil price scenarios, ranging from $85-$100 to a potential $130-$140 per barrel, depending on the duration of tensions. Precious metals experienced a surprising pullback due to competing market forces, but a recovery is anticipated.

iran.headline: 'Our Last Best Chance': Marco Rubio Defends US Strike On Iran's Nuclear Programme
iran.summary: The secretary of state framed the strike as not only necessary to impede Iran’s nuclear progress but also to dismantle its growing arsenal of conventional weapons.

iran.headline: F-15 Chase Of Iranian Drone Goes Viral, Triggers Air Defence Debate
iran.summary: In the video, the F 15 jet is seen trailing the drone before the footage cuts to a ground explosion and smoke rising near a facility in Erbil.

iran.headline: Global Markets | Japan's Nikkei reverses gains as Trump threatens more Iran attacks
iran.summary: Japanese stocks tumbled over 2% as US President Trump's vow of further strikes on Iran dashed hopes for a swift conflict resolution. The Nikkei reversed earlier gains, ending significantly lower. Investors booked profits amid uncertainty over the war's duration and potential impact on vital oil routes, leading to widespread selling across most sectors.

iran.headline: Global Markets | South Korean shares end 4.5% lower as Iran ceasefire hopes fade
iran.summary: South Korean shares plummeted over 4% on Thursday, with the KOSPI closing down 4.47% at 5,234.05. This decline was triggered by fading hopes for a swift end to the Iran conflict following U.S. President Trump's vow to continue military strikes. Concerns over the Strait of Hormuz and a supplementary budget to address energy security threats also weighed on the market.

iran.headline: Iran War, Cost Pressures Drag India Manufacturing To Four-Year Low: PMI Survey
iran.summary: Pranjul Bhandari, chief India economist at HSBC, said the easing of the manufacturing PMI to 53.9 reflected the impact of disruptions linked to the conflict in the Middle East, which are reverberating through the global economy.

general.headline: RBI grants approval to Emirates National Bank of Dubai to acquire up to 74% stake in RBL Bank
general.summary: RBL Bank becomes a foreign lender as the Reserve bank of India says it has no objection to Emirates National Bank of Dubai being classified as the promoter the Bank

general.headline: Amazon in talks to buy $9 billion satellite telecoms group: Report
general.summary: Amazon, founded by billionaire Jeff Bezos, is building a competing network called Amazon Leo, which has more than 200 satellites in orbit

general.headline: Oldest Tortoise Jonathan Alive, Veterinarian Confirms After Fake Death Post Spreads Online
general.summary: Jonathan, a Seychelles giant tortoise, is about 194 years old. He has lived through major historical events, including the coronation of Queen Victoria.

general.headline: Who Was Khushbaz Jattana? Congress Leader Dies In Haryana Road Accident After SUV Hit By Truck
general.summary: Jattana, aged 45, died on the spot along with his driver, Parminder Singh. His gunman was injured in the accident.

general.headline: ETMarkets Smart Talk | Renewables, defence to lead in FY27; infra and gas stocks offer high-risk opportunities: Siddarth Bhamre
general.summary: Siddarth Bhamre expects renewables and defence to lead market performance in FY27, supported by structural tailwinds. Infrastructure and gas stocks may offer high-risk opportunities post-correction. While geopolitical risks and weak earnings weigh on sentiment, FII flows could recover in the second half, with valuations improving but still dependent on earnings recovery.

general.headline: Axis Bank to deepen insurance bet with Rs 389 crore infusion in Axis Max Life; stake seen at 19.99%
general.summary: Axis Bank plans to invest Rs 389 crore in Axis Max Life, raising its combined stake to 19.99%, near the regulatory cap. The move strengthens its insurance play as the sector expands. The deal remains subject to approvals, while Max Financial will retain majority ownership of around 80%.

general.headline: Oil prices surge while Asian share prices rise moderately
general.summary: Benchmark U.S. crude rose 11.4% to $111.54 a barrel. The price of Brent crude, the international standard, jumped 7.8% to $109.03 per barrel

general.headline: The architecture of resilience: Why India’s private credit stands apart
general.summary: India’s private credit market stands resilient amid global stress due to its conservative fund structures, strict leverage norms, and closed-ended AIF framework. Unlike global peers facing liquidity and redemption pressures, India’s system aligns investor timelines with asset maturity, reducing systemic risks while offering significant growth headroom in an underpenetrated market.

general.headline: Crypto Price Today: Macro worries, geopolitical risks weigh on Bitcoin, altcoins as Good Friday trade remains lacklustre
general.summary: Crypto markets remained subdued on Good Friday as macro concerns and geopolitical tensions weighed on sentiment. Bitcoin hovered near $66,000 after recent declines, while altcoins traded flat. Analysts flagged persistent risk-off sentiment, weak ETF flows and technical resistance levels, indicating a near-term range-bound trend with a bearish bias.

general.headline: 'Routine Glitch, Familiar Problem': NASA Artemis II Livestream Shows Astronaut Reporting Microsoft Outlook Issue In Orbit
general.summary: An Artemis II astronaut reported two versions of Microsoft Outlook malfunctioning on the spacecraft, prompting Mission Control to remotely troubleshoot the issue.

general.headline: Buy, Sell Or Hold: Gujarat Gas, BPCL, Ultratech Cement, Paradeep Phosphates — Ask Profit
general.summary: Should you add shares of Gujarat Gas Ltd.?Should you hold shares ofBharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.at the current market price?

general.headline: Canada OCI Application Mandate Explained — Here's What It Means For Indians
general.summary: Canada has made in-person submission with mandatory biometrics compulsory for OCI applications from April 5, 2026.

general.headline: CBSE Rejects QR Code 'Rickroll' Claims, Says Exam Paper Security Not Compromised
general.summary: This clarification arrives in the wake of QR codes featured on recent CBSE exam papers, specifically the Class 12 History examination conducted on March 30.

general.headline: Have A PPF Account? How Investment Before April 5 Will Maximise The Returns
general.summary: Investing in PPF before April 5 each year ensures maximum interest earnings and can significantly increase your final corpus.

general.headline: Bank of Baroda Q4 update: PSU lender reports 14% YoY growth in global business; deposits rise 12%
general.summary: State-owned Bank of Baroda reported a 14% year-on-year rise in global business to Rs 30.78 lakh crore in Q4FY26, led by steady growth in deposits. Global deposits increased 12%, while domestic deposits rose 13%. The figures are provisional and part of the bank’s quarterly update, with final audited numbers to be announced alongside its earnings release.

general.headline: Petroleum Secretary met State Chief Secretaries, addressed concerns about LPG for autos, migrant workers
general.summary: The Petroleum Secretary sought states consider targeted distribution of 5 KG FTL cylinder, autos that can run both on petrol, LPG considering using the former.

general.headline: Concerns settled, share buybacks could flow via exchanges again
general.summary: Sebi is proposing to bring back share buybacks via stock exchanges. This reverses a decision made a year ago. New tax rules have resolved earlier concerns about fairness and tax distortions. Public shareholders will now be taxed on actual capital gains, similar to market sales. This change aims to ensure equal participation for all shareholders.

general.headline: RBI curbs widen dollar-rupee forwards-NDF spread
general.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has banned banks from offering or rebooking rupee non-deliverable forward contracts. This move widens the gap between domestic and overseas currency markets. Banks are now selling dollars locally and buying them abroad. This action aims to curb speculative trading and support the Indian rupee against the US dollar.

general.headline: HDFC Bank penalises 12 execs for role in mis-selling AT1 bonds
general.summary: HDFC Bank has penalized at least 12 senior and mid-level executives, including Ashish Parthasarthy, for their alleged role in the mis-selling of Credit Suisse AT1 bonds. This action follows previous sackings and suspensions, with the bank attributing the issue to technical documentation and regulatory interpretation lapses rather than fraud.

general.headline: Rupee logs biggest gain in 12 years
general.summary: The Indian rupee saw a strong surge on Thursday, gaining nearly 1.8% against the dollar. This appreciation followed new regulations by the Reserve Bank of India aimed at curbing speculative trading. The currency reached its best single-day gain since September 2013. Traders anticipate the rupee to trade within a specific range next week as banks adjust their positions.

general.headline: Pharma calls in sick after Trump’s 100% tariff threat
general.summary: Pharma stocks fell Thursday after reports of US President Donald Trump mulling 100% tariffs on imported branded and patented medicine makers spooked investors in a weak market.

general.headline: Global Market Check | Asian shares edge higher as volatile week draws to an end
general.summary: Asian stocks traded higher on Friday, supported by easing oil prices and improved global risk sentiment amid Middle East tensions. MSCI’s Asia Pacific Index rose up to 1% after Wall Street rebounded. Hopes of partial normalization in oil boosted liquidity outlook. Focus now shifts to key US nonfarm payrolls data, even as several regional markets remained shut for holidays.

general.headline: Rupee at 100 will be a harsh check on India’s ambitions
general.summary: India's rupee has hit record lows, exacerbated by Middle East conflict and domestic economic fragilities. The Reserve Bank of India's currency controls backfired, signaling a loss of confidence. A rupee at 100 threatens to increase living costs, strain government finances, and impact aspirational spending, posing a harsh check on the nation's ambitions.

general.headline: Trump Will Impose 100% Tariff On Some Patented Drugs
general.summary: Duties for products made by certain larger companies will take effect in 120 days, while items from smaller manufacturers won't be hit for another 180 days, according to a White House statement.

general.headline: Stock market holiday today for Good Friday 2026: Are NSE, BSE and MCX open or closed on April 3? Check now
general.summary: NSE Holidays for Good Friday 2026: NSE, BSE Holiday Today | Domestic stock exchanges NSE and BSE are closed for trading today on account of the Good Friday holiday. Commodity exchanges MCX and NCDEX will also remain shut.

general.headline: Gold, Silver Prices On April 3: Check Rates In Mumbai, Delhi, Chennai, Kolkata, Bangalore
general.summary: In the international market, spot silver plummeted by $5.49, or 7.32%, to $69.57 per ounce, while gold declined $166.79, or 3.51%, to $4,591.52 per ounce.

general.summary: Indian benchmark indices have fallen over 15% so far this year, entering a correction phase as higher transaction taxes, geopolitical tensions and currency weakness weighed on sentiment.

general.summary: Japan's Nikkei 225 Stock Average climbed 1.4% and Korea's Kospi jumped 2.7% . Asian markets were closed on Friday in Australia, New Zealand, Hong Kong, Singapore, the Philippines and Indonesia.

general.summary: The S&P 500 Index closed 0.1% higher after swinging between a 1.5% loss and a 0.4% gain earlier on Thursday. The Nasdaq 100 Index rose 0.1%.

general.summary: There has been a delay in the delivery schedule of engines for the LCA Mk-1A variant, which was criticised by Chief of Air Staff Air Chief Marshal A.P. Singh during the last edition of the Aero India in Bengaluru

general.summary: Muzarabani's spell played a key role in restricting SRH to 226/8, with the Zimbabwe pacer finishing with figures of 4/41 in a high-scoring encounter at the Eden Gardens.

general.summary: Abhishek Sharma was at his rampaging best, scoring at a blistering strike-rate of 228.57 when he was dismissed in the 9th over.

general.summary: Lower SEZ duties may ease prices, boost supply and protect jobs, but impact may remain limited.

general.summary: This includes ₹2.58 lakh crore from customs duty, ₹3.38 lakh crore from excise duty, and ₹9.58 lakh crore from CGST

general.summary: The maintenance work is planned between Vijayanagar Metro Station and Attiguppe Metro Station on the Purple Line. As part of this work, train services will not operate for two hours on a key section of the route.

general.summary: On India's MCX, the gold futures slated for June delivery were trading 2.5% lower at about Rs 1.49 lakh per 10 gram.

general.summary: While many users expressed their views, one of the users pointed out remarks from Donald Trump, noting that he had recently claimed the economy was 'doing great'.

general.headline: Sanjay Dutt's Portrayal Of Chaudhry Aslam In Dhurandhar Praised By Former Pakistani Cop's Widow

general.headline: Why RBI is clamping down on FX arbitrage

The Reserve Bank of India is curbing speculative arbitrage trades that amplified currency pressure. general.headline: Rising yields amid Middle East crisis trigger Rs 17,689 cr FPI outflow from FAR G-Sec
general.summary: Global investors have withdrawn nearly Rs 17,700 crore from Indian government bonds since the Middle East conflict began, driven by rising risk aversion and inflation fears from soaring crude oil prices. This outflow has pushed domestic bond yields higher, with the benchmark 10-year bond exceeding 7% for the first time in over 20 months, making existing holdings less attractive.
